channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Venezuela deadly earthquake: 'Countless lives lost that in a normal country would have been rescued'
item.description: Gavin Lee is pleased to welcome Germania Rodriguez Paleo, American-Venezuelan journalist and commentator, former US chief reporter for the Daily Mail. She offers a forceful political interpretation of Venezuela's earthquake disaster through the lens of institutional collapse and democratic illegitimacy. Rodriguez Paleo argues that while the earthquake itself was a natural event, the scale of the resulting humanitarian catastrophe was profoundly shaped by decades of political decay.
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE

item.title: Middle East War: US and Iran trade strikes putting new strain on Mideast ceasefire
item.description: The United States and Iran traded military strikes after Washington on Friday accused Tehran of attacking a cargo ship, jeopardizing a fragile ceasefire as diplomats struggle to contain the Middle East war. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: US strikes Iran after Trump says ceasefire was violated
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Burkina Faso ruling junta cuts diplomatic ties with ex-ruler France
item.description: Burkina Faso's military junta on Friday cut diplomatic ties with former colonial power France, accusing Paris of acting against its interests and backing subversive networks and militants in the Sahel. The move deepens the ruling authorities' break with Western allies since Captain Ibrahim Traore seized power in a 2022 coup.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Senegal crush 10-man Iraq to remain in hunt for knockout spot
item.description: Senegal kept their hopes of qualifying for the World Cup knockouts alive with a thumping 5-0 win over Iraq on Friday as they finished third in Group I behind France and Norway. The Lions of Teranga remain in the hunt for one of the last eight spots in the ⁠round of 32, while Iraq's tournament is over.
item.source: Getty Images via AFP - MICHAEL REAVES
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Dembélé scores sensational hat-trick as France beat Norway to top Group I
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Dembélé scores sensational hat-trick as France beat Norway to top group
item.description: Ballon d'Or winner Ousmane Dembélé scored a magnificent first-half hat-trick, his first with Les Bleus, as France beat a second-string Norway 4-1 on Friday to seal top spot in Group I. Star striker Erling Haaland was one of several players rested by the Norway coach ahead of the World Cup knockouts stage.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump threatens 100% tariffs on Europe if countries impose tax on US digital services
item.description: US President Donald Trump threatened Friday to impose 100 percent tariffs on European countries that implement a digital services tax, even if it means canceling existing trade agreements.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Togo's parliamentary reforms: unconstitutional
item.description: In tonight's programme, Togo’s switch to a parliamentary system is under scrutiny, after a regional court ruled the reforms: unconstitutional. Also once a jewel of Ethiopia’s tourism scene, Lake Tana is now caught in the shadow of conflict. And archaeologists are racing to preserve the ancient ruins of Cyrene and Apollonia, as conflict and natural disaster put these UNESCO treasures at risk.
item.dc:creator: Clarisse FORTUNÉ

item.title: Venezuelans search for missing as earthquake death toll climbs
item.description: The death toll from the twin earthquakes in Venezuela continued to rise on Friday, with at least 920 dead and more than 51,000 missing, as distress grew among Venezuelans. The 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes that struck late Wednesday left a landscape of devastation, with countless collapsed buildings, particularly in La Guaira, a coastal town near Caracas, where residents are criticising the government's lack of action in rescue operations.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Europe on high alert as deadly heat set to move east and south
item.description: The unprecedented heatwave that has been hitting Europe since the beginning of the week is spreading east and south across the continent, leading to the cancellation of events as temperatures exceed 35C and hospitals are becoming overwhelmed with demand.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Morocco footballer Hakimi to appeal rape trial order
item.description: Moroccan footballer Achraf Hakimi, whose case was remanded for trial on rape charges on June 19, on Friday lodged an appeal with France's Court of Cassation, France's top court for civil and criminal matters. The Versailles Court of Appeals upheld the decision to refer the Moroccan and PSG defender to a criminal court on charges of raping a woman in 2023.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Brexit Britain: Sunlit Uplands or a Toad in the Harrow?
item.description:
Political change in Britain has dominated the headlines, with Keir Starmer’s resignation paving the way for Andy Burnham, as the country also marks ten years since the Brexit vote.
Fresh peace talks between the US and Iran have begun amid continuing tensions and sharply different accounts of progress from both sides.
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE

item.title: Middle East War: Lebanon, Israel and US sign framework agreement in Washington
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Middle East war: Lebanon, Israel and US sign framework agreement in Washington
item.description: ⁠US Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced Friday a framework agreement between ‌Israel ⁠and Lebanon ‌following talks held ⁠in Washington. Nada Hamadeh, ambassador of Lebanon to the United States described the framework as "a first step on the road of restoring Lebanese sovereignty and territorial integrity".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: France top World Cup group as Dembélé's first-half treble downs second-string Norway
item.description: Ballon ⁠d'Or ⁠winner Ousmane Dembélé scored three goals in just 25 minutes as France defeated Norway's reserves 4-1 to seal top spot in Group I on Friday, while Senegal kept their hopes of a World Cup knockouts spot alive with a crushing victory over Iraq. Read our liveblog to see how the matches unfolded.
item.source: © Winslow Townson, Imagn Images via Reuters
item.dc:creator: Benjamin DODMAN

item.title: More than a month into DR Congo Ebola outbreak, doctors warn 'this epidemic will last'
item.description: While France confirmed its first case of Ebola, reports of people successfully treated for the condition are also emerging from Mongbwalu, the epicentre of the epidemic in the eastern part of the DRC. Although successful treatments are sparking hope among teams on the ground, health professionals remain fearful about the continued progression of cases.
item.dc:creator: The FRANCE 24 Observers

item.title: 'Living in a warming world requires practical solutions that save lives, don't harm our environment'
item.description: As Europe confronts increasingly frequent and intense heatwaves, the conversation around climate adaptation is shifting from environmental policy to public health, education, and social infrastructure. Haxie Meyers-Belkin is pleased to welcome Caradee Wright, Chief Specialist Scientist at the South African Medical Research Council leading the Climate and Health Research Programme. She argues that schools have become one of the clearest frontlines of climate change. Her analysis moves beyond the immediate debate over air conditioning to present a more systemic vision of heat resilience, combining architecture, behavioural adaptation, public awareness, and educational policy.Rather than framing extreme heat as a temporary disruption, Wright treats it as a defining condition of the coming decades that demands structural change. She highlights children's unique physiological vulnerability, questions whether historic school buildings remain fit for a warming climate, and argues that protecting education requires redesigning both physical spaces and institutional routines.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: In some Mexican regions, endemic violence dampens World Cup excitement
item.description: Much of Mexico has been celebrating the World Cup with joyful outdoor gatherings, filling streets, plazas and fan zones in the country's three host cities — Mexico City, Guadalajara and Monterrey — since the tournament kickoff on June 11. But in parts of the country plagued by cartel violence, the event is experienced differently: with fear.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Over a million immigrants risk deportation from US after landmark decision
item.description: The US Supreme Court has handed the Trump Administration another win on its policies aggressively cracking down on immigration and allowing mass deportations. In the conservative majority's 6-3 decision -the justices ruled that Washington can lift the temporary protected status of hundreds of thousands of Haitian and Syrian migrants in the United States. That decision paves the way for those TPS holders to be sent back to their home countries.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France introduces measures to limit the heatwave's impact on health services
item.description: A punishing heatwave that has suffocated large parts of France for days is now shifting east. With highs of 38 degrees Celsius forecast for cities like Paris, Strasbourg and Lyon. With hospitals overwhelmed, authorities have banned evening alcohol sales and public consumption in the capital, while a number of events, including this weekend's pride march, have been cancelled or postponed.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Before and after images reveal scale of devastation
item.description: Satellite images taken in La Guaira State, the area hardest hit by the double earthquake that struck Venezuela on Wednesday, June 24, have revealed the scale of the devastation in the country. As the search for survivors goes on the death toll from the quakes continues to rise, with at least 589 people killed and nearly 3,000 injured.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Former Trump national security adviser Bolton pleads guilty in classified files case
item.description: John Bolton, a former national security adviser to US President Donald Trump who has since become one of his most vocal critics, pleaded guilty Friday to withholding classified documents related to national defence.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
